mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the effective federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and signals policy accommodation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.38% in June 2019 with market pricing an imminent cut ahead of the July 2019 FOMC meeting
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20-day forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month moving average of UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points above its trailing 12-month low (a Sahm-rule-style threshold), it signals labor market inflection consistent with recession onset, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of UNRATE exceeds its trailing 12-month low by more than 0.3 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% in June 2019, near cycle lows, with the threshold not yet triggered
failure_condition: In periods where the Sahm-style threshold is triggered, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the signal fails to precede any of the historically labeled recession-adjacent drawdowns in the 2005-2019 sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in retail sales growth (RSAFS) and a decline in housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal broad-based consumer and residential investment weakness that is not captured by either series alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS year-over-year growth decelerates below its 6-month average while HOUST declines more than 5% month-over-month, both conditions concurrent
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value 1269K units in May 2019, revised to 1293K, showing modest softening
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-HOUST weakness signal produces negative 20-day forward returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or a model using RSAFS and HOUST independently achieves equal or higher Rank IC than the joint interaction term.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) growth occurring during a late-cycle expansion regime signals impending margin compression and capex pullback among manufacturers, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; the same deceleration during an early-cycle recovery regime instead reflects transitory restocking noise and does not predict negative returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth rate turns negative
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 109.5645 in May 2019 revised down to 102.392, indicating manufacturing softness amid trade tensions
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth episodes occurring more than 6 years after the last recession trough fail to produce below-median 60-day forward returns, or the effect direction is identical in early-cycle recovery periods (no regime-dependent flip detected).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is declining while CPIAUCSL inflation remains stable or rising, the resulting drop in the ex-post real policy rate signals a stimulative monetary stance that is more powerful than nominal rate cuts alone, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage 255.305 in June 2019 with FEDFUNDS at 2.38% ahead of anticipated rate cuts
failure_condition: The real-rate interaction term (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation trend) shows Rank IC no greater than the FEDFUNDS-only model over any 2-year rolling window, indicating no incremental interaction value.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects ongoing labor market expansion and consumer income growth, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as an always-on trend signal independent of regime.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage 151308K in June 2019 revised to 150838K, continuing multi-year expansion trend
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing PAYEMS growth and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive PAYEMS growth periods do not show statistically higher forward returns than negative growth periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 8% signals a sharp contraction in residential investment activity that historically precedes broader demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days once the threshold is crossed.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage 1269K units May 2019 revised to 1293K, within normal range but sensitive to future declines
failure_condition: Instances where HOUST declines more than 8% month-over-month are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 forward returns in more than 55% of cases, invalidating the threshold's predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening-to-easing transition regime, a combination of decelerating industrial production growth (INDPRO) and slowing payroll growth (PAYEMS) jointly signals the Fed's anticipated policy pivot is arriving too late to prevent a demand slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days that would not be predicted by either series alone.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth turns negative while PAYEMS 3-month average job gains fall below 150,000, concurrent with FEDFUNDS at or near cycle peak
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised down to 102.392 in May 2019 while FEDFUNDS remained at 2.38% ahead of expected cuts
regime_hint: monetary policy pivot from tightening to easing
failure_condition: The joint INDPRO-PAYEMS-FEDFUNDS trigger produces negative 60-day forward returns in fewer than 55% of historically triggered instances, or a model using INDPRO and PAYEMS independently matches or exceeds the joint interaction model's Rank IC.
